description_long: This is a pair of metal pliers with a utilitarian, industrial design, featuring a robust, slightly curved handle and a circular locking mechanism. The main body is constructed from a single piece of metal, with a pivot joint connecting the two arms, allowing for a scissor-like motion. The jaws are relatively short and blunt, with a flat gripping surface, and the tool includes a distinctive ring-shaped latch for securing the handles together when not in use. The surface of the pliers is matte and shows signs of wear, with a muted, weathered metallic finish. There are no visible rubber or plastic components, and the tool appears to be designed for durability and heavy-duty use. No branding or manufacturer markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A sturdy, metal pair of pliers with a flat jaw and a circular locking latch, finished in a matte, weathered metallic color.
description_view_0: The main body and the circular locking latch are clearly visible, showing the full length of the handles and the blunt jaws.
description_view_1: A side profile emphasizing the thinness of the handles and the alignment of the jaws, with the latch partially obscured.
description_view_2: The pliers are shown from an angle highlighting the curvature of the handles and the open position of the jaws and latch.
description_view_3: A direct side view, focusing on the thickness of the metal and the alignment of the pivot joint.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, slightly curved metal handles with no insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, blunt jaws with a smooth gripping surface, lacking serrations or wire-cutting features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed pivot joint allowing standard scissor-like motion, with no adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible texturing or pattern.
